{{Quote|We're in attack position now, sir.|Commander Merrejk, informing Admiral Firmus Piett of the ''Executor''{{'s}} status during the Battle of Endor|<ref name="ROTJ" />}}
'''Merrejk''' was a [[Human/Legends|Human]] [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the height of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. As an elite [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Imperial]] [[Spy/Legends|spy]] and [[Scout/Legends|scout]], he often posed as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] making supply runs to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]], monitoring [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] and criminal activity. By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]], Merrejk was assigned to [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], where he served on the [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] under the command of [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]]. During the early stages of the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Merrejk notified Piett that the ''Executor'' had reached attack position, to which Piett responded that the [[Warship/Legends|warship]] would hold its position in order to allow the secretly operational [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]] to wreak havoc upon the [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]]. The battle ended with the destruction of both the ''Executor'' and the [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]].

==Biography==
[[File:ExecutorShuttle-ESB.jpg|thumb|right|260px|Merrejk was stationed aboard the ''Executor'' during the Battle of Endor.]]
The [[Human/Legends|Human]] Merrejk was a [[Commander/Legends|commander]] of the [[Imperial Navy/Legends|Imperial Navy]] during the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]]. An elite [[Spy/Legends|spy]] and [[Scout/Legends|scout]] of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], he often masqueraded as a [[Freighter/Legends|freighter]] [[Captain/Legends|captain]] making supply runs to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. His primary duties included watching for illegal [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] and other criminal activity.<ref name="Death Star" /> By [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]],<ref name="New Essential Chronology">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]] to [[4 ABY/Legends|4 ABY]].</ref> Merrejk was assigned aboard the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Dark Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader's]] [[Flagship/Legends|flagship]], the [[Executor-class Star Dreadnought/Legends|Super Star Destroyer]] [[Executor/Legends|''Executor'']], during its watch-guard role of the [[Death Star II/Legends|second Death Star]] [[Battle station/Legends|battle station]], under construction in [[Orbit/Legends|orbit]] over the remote [[Endor/Legends|Forest Moon of Endor]].<ref name="ROTJ" />

During the early stages of the pivotal [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]], Merrejk was stationed aboard the ''Executor''{{'s}} [[Bridge (starship)/Legends|bridge]] under the command of [[Fleet Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] [[Firmus Piett/Legends|Firmus Piett]], where he informed his [[Commanding officer/Legends|superior officer]] that the [[Warship/Legends|warship]], at the head of the massive Imperial Fleet waiting in ambush of the recently arrived [[Alliance Fleet/Legends|Alliance Fleet]], had reached attack position. However, Piett ordered Merrejk to hold their current position, revealing to a nearby flabbergasted Commander [[Gherant/Legends|Gherant]], another of the [[Super Star Destroyer/Legends|Super Star Destroyer's]] [[Imperial officer/Legends|officers]], that the ''Executor''{{'s}} duty was only to keep the Rebel Fleet from escaping, for [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] had a specially designed present waiting to destroy them—the fully operational [[Superlaser/Legends|superlaser]] of the uncompleted [[Death Star/Legends|Death Star]]. The battle ended with the ''Executor'' colliding with the Death Star and both being destroyed.<ref name="ROTJ" />

==Personality and traits==
As an expert spy and scout, Merrejk would often use his skills in service to the Empire to keep tabs on Rebel and criminal activity, frequently posing as a freighter captain making trips to the Outer Rim.<ref name="Death Star" /> Merrejk had [[Color/Legends|brown]] [[Hair/Legends|hair]], bluish [[Eye/Legends|eyes]], and light skin.<ref name="ROTJ" />

==Equipment==
Commander Merrejk wore the black variant of the standard-issue [[Imperial officer's uniform/Legends|Imperial officer's uniform]], sans [[Rank insignia plaque/Legends|rank insignia]], and a chrome [[Utility belt/Legends|utility belt]] during the Battle of Endor.<ref name="ROTJ" />

==Behind the scenes==
Merrejk first appeared in the [[1983]] film [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Star Wars'': Episode VI ''Return of the Jedi'']] as an unidentified character extra, portrayed by an uncredited actor.<ref name="ROTJ" /> His single line, alerting [[Admiral/Legends|Admiral]] Firmus Piett of the ''Executor''{{'s}} ready status, is unique to the movie and not included in the ''Return of the Jedi'' [[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|novelization]], in which Piett independently determines that the warship will hold at its given position.<ref name="ROTJ novel">[[Star Wars: Return of the Jedi (novelization)|''Star Wars Episode VI: Return of the Jedi'' novelization]]</ref> The Merrejk character was first identified by name and provided a brief backstory on the [http://www.starwarsccg.org/cardlists/images/starwars/DeathStarII-Dark/large/commandermerrejk.gif "Commander Merrejk" card] as part of the [[July]] [[2000]] ''[[Death Star II Limited]]'' expansion set of the [[Star Wars Customizable Card Game|''Star Wars'' Customizable Card Game]].<ref name="Death Star" />

While no official ''[[Star Wars]]'' source discusses Merrejk's fate during the Battle of Endor, in which the ''Executor'' is ultimately destroyed,<ref name="ROTJ" /> [[Leland Chee]], keeper of [[Lucasfilm]]'s [[Holocron continuity database]], when asked whether Merrejk was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during the ''Executor''{{'s}} destruction, has stated on his personal [http://www.facebook.com/permalink.php?story_fbid=231839790170288&id=201820953172172 Facebook fan page] that he's "Not sure why we'd go out of the way to say he's alive," and that it is "Best to presume dead unless proven otherwise."<ref name="Chee Facebook">{{Facebook|noprofile=1|author=[[Leland Chee|Chee, Leland]] ("Keeper of the Holocron")|date=2011-07-07|quote=Not sure why we'd go out of the way to say he's alive. Best to presume dead unless proven otherwise.|url=permalink.php?story_fbid=231839790170288&id=201820953172172|dateaccessed=2012-12-12|nobackup=true}}</ref>
